target.name: QuantumScape
target.founder: Jagdeep Singh, Tim Holme, Fritz Prinz

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
QuantumScape, a public Climate & Energy company, was valued at $5B+ following $2B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 10 climate-tech companies at similar valuation tiers, comprising 1 public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 9 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Form Energy.

The peer-median method, the primary applicable triangulation approach, yielded an implied valuation of $3.4B based on the median of the 10-company cohort. The peer set ranges from $1.0B to $6.0B, with sample peers valued as follows: TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials at $6.0B, Helion at $5.4B, Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B, and Form Energy at $3.5B. This method reflects current market pricing for similarly-staged, climate-focused capital-intensive ventures.

The triangulated valuation range of $2.9B to $3.9B, centered at $3.4B, sits materially below QuantumScape's stated $5B+ valuation. The discrepancy likely reflects company-specific factors—including technology differentiation, IP position, or path to commercialization timelines—not fully captured by sector peer benchmarking. With only one applicable method and limited public comp data in this sector, valuation confidence is constrained. Key uncertainties include commercialization progress relative to peers and the appropriate stage-adjustment premium warranted by public status.
